title: Matelea flavidula
taxonomy: Matelea flavidula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Matelea, which is a group of tropical plants native to Central and South America. Matelea flavidula is a type of vine or climber, characterized by its twining stems and clusters of small, yellowish flowers. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central America, specifically in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. Matelea flavidula is a relatively understudied species, but it is valued for its unique characteristics and potential uses in traditional medicine.

This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions, including pain and oxidative stress.
